title: Doll X Picnic Table and Bench Set
summary: A doll-scale X-leg picnic table with two matching benches, all from four 1x2s. Every joint is a 30° miter — a fun exercise in repeatable angle cuts.
description:
This is a miniature X-frame picnic table with two benches, scaled for 18" dolls and built entirely from 1x2 stock — four 8-foot boards cover the whole set. It's a charming gift and a genuinely good practice piece, because almost every cut is a 30° angle and getting a clean, matched set teaches you a lot about miter-saw setups and stop blocks.

The design is the classic crossed-X picnic bench in tiny form: legs cross in an X under each end, with slatted tops and seats. Pay attention to the cut list's distinction between 'not parallel' and 'parallel' angle cuts — the X legs have non-parallel 30° ends (the angles tilt opposite ways so the legs splay into an X), while the cross members have parallel ends. Mixing these up gives you legs that won't sit flat.

Assembly is glue and brad nails, and the source is emphatic about not skipping glue on the legs — on parts this small the glue is what actually holds, and the brads just clamp it while it cures. Build the tabletop by nailing the supports to the underside of the top slats, then attach the longer legs to the insides of the supports, then the shorter legs. Repeat the whole process at smaller scale for the two benches. Sand well and finish for a display-worthy set.

tools.note: Every joint is a 30° cut — a stop block keeps the many repeats identical.

materials.name: 1x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Covers the table and both benches.

materials.name: Wood glue
materials.note: Do NOT skip the glue on the legs.

cutList.note: Both ends 30°, NOT parallel (long point to long point).
Both ends 30°, NOT parallel (long point to long point).

steps.title: Cut all the angled parts
steps.body: Cut all the pieces, watching the cut list's distinction between 'not parallel' and 'parallel' 30° ends. The X-leg supports have non-parallel ends so the legs splay into an X; the leg pieces have parallel ends. A stop block keeps the many repeated cuts identical.

steps.title: Attach the tabletop supports
steps.body: Glue and nail the supports to the underside of the tabletop slats. These carry the X-legs and hold the top together.

steps.title: Attach the longer legs
steps.body: Attach the longer legs to the insides of the supports, forming one arm of each X.

steps.title: Attach the shorter legs
steps.body: Attach the remaining shorter legs to complete the X on each end. Don't forget the glue on these legs — on parts this small the glue does the holding and the brads just clamp it while it cures.

steps.title: Build the two benches
steps.body: Repeat the whole process at smaller scale for the two benches: supports under the seat slats, longer legs, then shorter legs, gluing every joint.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand every edge smooth and finish the set for display. It's small enough that finishing is quick.

images.alt: Doll-scale X-leg picnic table with two benches
